sourceMovie.title: The Omega Man
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Omega Man works as a science fiction movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The science fiction and action blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: I Am Legend
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Omega Man clicked for you, I Am Legend (2007) is a strong next stop: both trade in science fiction and drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: World War Z
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Omega Man clicked for you, World War Z (2013) is a strong next stop: both trade in science fiction and action, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Omega Man?
faq.answer: Try I Am Legend, World War Z, Maze Runner: The Scorch Trials—each shares DNA with The Omega Man while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Omega Man?
faq.answer: The Omega Man (1971) is best described as Science Fiction, Action, Drama, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Omega Man worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this science fiction lane.
